issues.sample: The transmission has several issues, hard shifting, shuddering and shaking but the one issue I know is a safety issue is the lunging. When moving the shifter from Park to drive, my truck has lunges, 3 different times now. If my foot wasn’t securely and pressing firmly on the brak

WHEN I START THE CAR, IT MAKES A RATTLE  NOISE AND WHEN THE ENGINE WARMS UP, IT GOES AWAY, BUT EVERY TIME I ACCELERATE, YOU CAN HEAR THE RATTLE AGAIN AND WHEN I REMOVE MY FOOT FROM THE ACCELERATION PEDAL, THE RATTLE GOES AWAY AND LIKE THAT EVERY TIME I ACCELERATE.  I TOOK THE CAR

EXTENDED CAB MODEL, REAR DRIVER DOOR INTERMITTENTLY INDICATES DOOR AJAR. ALERT APPEARS WHILE IN MOTION, USUALLY AFTER A BUMP IN THE ROAD. THIS HAPPENS AFTER THE DOOR IS CLOSED BY GRIPPING MIDWAY DOWN THE FRAME. THIS WARNING CAN BE PREVENTED IF THE DOOR IS CLOSED BY GRIPPING THE T

THE ADAPTIVE CURISE CONTROL DOES NOT WORK LIKE OTHER VEHICLES. WHEN THE CRUISE CONTROL IS ACTIVE IT KEEPS THE PROPER DISTANCE BETWEEN CARS UNTIL THE CAR IN FRONT SLOWS DOWN AND THEN WHEN THERE IS ABOUT TWO CAR LENGTHS IT RELEASES THE BRAKES MAKING YOU HAVE TO JAM ON THE BRAKES BE

REAR PASSENGER SIDE DOOR IS HARD TO OPEN AND CLOSE. MY SEVEN YEAR OLD CAN'T OPEN IT WITHOUT HELP ON THAT SIDE. WARNING LIGHT COMES ON WHILE DRIVING SOMETIMES WHEN IT IS CLOSED. AFTER PARKING THE PROBLEM CAUSES THE REAR TAILGATE TO STAY ON TO DRAIN BATTERY BECAUSE TRUCK REGISTERS

narrative.headline: Power train leads 2020 Ford Ranger complaints
narrative.summary: Of 117 owner complaints filed with NHTSA, power train problems are the most common at 41 reports (35%), followed by the engine at 14 reports (12%) and the electrical system at 11 reports (9%). No fires were reported in any category. The service brakes stand out for safety: 9 reports (8%) include 3 crashes and 4 injuries. Buyers should focus their inspection on the transmission and drivetrain, and test the brakes carefully.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: The single largest complaint area: 41 reports, or 35% of all complaints, with no crashes or fires reported. Check the transmission and drivetrain carefully on a test drive.

issues.sample: WHEN I START THE CAR, IT MAKES A RATTLE  NOISE AND WHEN THE ENGINE WARMS UP, IT GOES AWAY, BUT EVERY TIME I ACCELERATE, YOU CAN HEAR THE RATTLE AGAIN AND WHEN I REMOVE MY FOOT FROM THE ACCELERATION PEDAL, THE RATTLE GOES AWAY AND LIKE THAT EVERY TIME I ACCELERATE.  I TOOK THE CAR
issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: The second most common concern: 14 reports (12% of complaints), with no crashes or fires reported. Have the engine inspected before buying.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: 11 reports (9% of complaints), with no crashes or fires reported. Verify the electronics and warning lights all work as expected.

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: 9 reports (8% of complaints), but the most safety-relevant: 3 crashes and 4 injuries reported. Test the brakes carefully before buying.
